title: Candide
author: Voltaire
action: Context: The retreat from futile, abstract theorizing to the management of one’s own immediate domain.

title: A Tale of Two Cities
author: Charles Dickens
content: “It was the best of times, it was the worst of times, it was the age of wisdom, it was the age of foolishness...”
action: Context: The observation that systemic contradictions often exist simultaneously.

title: Civil Disobedience
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “If the machine of government is of such a nature that it requires you to be the agent of injustice to another, then, I say, break the law.”
action: Context: The definition of the individual's moral override mechanism in a corrupted system.

title: The Age of Reason
author: Thomas Paine
content: “My own mind is my own church.”
action: Context: The declaration of absolute intellectual independence from institutional dogma.

title: Faust
author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe
content: “Two souls, alas, are dwelling in my breast; and one is striving to forsake its brother.”
action: Context: The inherent friction between competing internal impulses.
